description: A standard bottle of strong spirits. Good for drinking, or throwing into a fire.
A pile of gold coins. Standard currency across the continent.
A highly addictive and dangerous fey narcotic. Proceed with extreme caution.
A crystalline stimulant crafted by dwarven alchemists to keep miners awake for days.
A fast-acting alchemical liquid that makes the world seem to stand still.
A glowing pink substance that induces overwhelming, unearned happiness.
A classic red potion that knits wounds and mends flesh upon consumption.
A viscous, pale liquid that aggressively expands the consumer's mass.
A leather skin full of fresh water. Drink to quench thirst, or pour out as a fire-quenching splash.
A robust glass flask containing a highly corrosive green acid.
A swirl of white vapor trapped in a fragile glass sphere.
Dark, angry clouds and flashes of miniature lightning violently swirl inside this reinforced jar.
A foul-smelling, incredibly slippery byproduct of goblin 'cooking'.
A small pouch of enchanted desert sand that bursts into a blinding cloud when released.
A magically preserved shard of true ice that freezes whatever it shatters against.
A radiant ointment that causes the user's skin to emit a comforting, holy light.
A luxurious, sweet-smelling cream guaranteed to clear complexions and win hearts.
A tiny, discreet vial filled with a deadly, dark-purple toxin.
An incredibly potent adhesive that permanently bonds almost anything together.
A refined ingot of strong metal, ready for the forge.
A sturdy piece of chopped wood, useful for crafting or starting a fire.
A heavy, solid chunk of rough-hewn stone.
A simple wooden arrow with an iron tip.
A lead bullet for firearms.

display_name: Head of the King of Skarsgaard

description: A humble round head of cabbage. Hearty peasant fare.
A waxy wheel of farmhouse cheese, sharp and filling.
A round cottage loaf, its crust scored with a baker's cross.
A whole roasted fowl, dripping and golden. A feast for one.
A crisp red apple.
A humble turnip. Better than an empty belly.
A fresh river trout, its scales still bright.
A wooden bowl of thick pottage, the everyday stew of the poor.
A golden-crusted meat pie. Best not ask what's inside.
A papery brown onion, sharp enough to bring a tear.
A sturdy hooped wooden barrel.
A wooden crate for storing goods.
A woven wicker basket.
A burlap sack heavy with grain.
A clay amphora for oil or wine.
A wooden bucket.
A round wooden table.
A long wooden bench.
A simple wooden stool.
A straw-stuffed bed.
A traveller's rolled bedroll.
A crackling campfire ringed with stones.
An iron brazier of glowing coals.
A heavy iron cauldron.
A burning torch.
An ornate woven rug.
A bearskin rug.
A loose pile of hay.
A scatter of old bones and a skull.
A dark stain of dried blood.
A fresh mound of turned earth.
A wooden trapdoor with an iron ring.
An iron grate over a drain.
A heavy round grinding stone.
A coil of stout rope.
A wooden hand cart.
A spoked wooden cartwheel.
A bundle of chopped firewood.
A stone well of dark water.
A wooden water trough.
A carved stone altar, stained dark.
A nailed wooden coffin.
A barred iron cage.
A grim heap of skulls.
A peaked canvas tent.
A cut tree stump.
A mossy fallen log.
A round leafy shrub.
A large mossy boulder.
A canvas-awninged market stall.
A single hand-rolled cigarette.
A wooden smoking pipe.
A brass snuff box engraved with a dragon and the word SNUFF.
